Thank you. Alright, so as you heard, I'm Guillermo, G, Gugliermo, Guerrilla Mo, telemarketers are usually the best. They call me the best things and also William, because Guillermo is Spanish for William. I made a little presentation because I fear what I'm doing right here right now. So this will keep me on track. I've been freelancing now for five years. And as you can see in my third bullet, there I have a huge fear of public speaking. I just want to make that clear. Now, the biggest thing that has made me, the one thing that's helped me grow my copywriting career, is following my fear. Because I, like Paul, am a major introvert. I am slowly coming out of the shell, always very shy. And honestly, the fact that I'm okay with giving this talk and here and still alive, is a testament to how it's grown my copywriting business. So what I want to convey to you guys is that ultimately fear can be an ally. It's not something to be avoided. Because when you make it your ally and you make fear something that you follow and you actually seek out, it will grow not only you personally as a person, but it will grow your business and that will translate into every area of your life. If you don't, then fear will be something that imprisons you. And it'll be the warden basically of your mind and you'll just, you'll live year after year, living the same year. So you'll have lived, say, five years, but you're no further on year five than you were on year one. And now just to be clear, the kind of fear I'm talking about is emotional fear. Not the kind of fear that if you're on African safari, you know, taking a picture of a rhinoceros and it starts charging at you, that's the fear of when you're in physical danger. I'm talking about the emotional fear of, you know, if you're cold calling a client the fear of rejection. If you're going to market yourself, the fear of feeling vulnerable. What is your family going to say? What will potential clients who see your marketing, what will they say? I'm talking about the fear of starting your copywriting business and leaving your full-time job. And the fear that tells you, well, I fall flat on my face and then my spouse will leave me, take the dog, and I'll have to figure out how many ways I can say welcome to Walmart. So that's the kind of fear I'm talking about. So now my story with fear and how I got to here where I am today facing this fear, I was working three jobs. I had gotten the copywriting course five years earlier from AWAI. I didn't do anything with it. Well, I did. I sent in my restaurant letter and back then you can get feedback on your letter. So I sent it in. I was excited. I think I had done a bang-up job, followed everything they said. I got it back. I saw the letter, opened it up, and I got one of the worst scores you can get, I think it was. So basically my restaurant letter sucked. And so five years passed because I thought it wasn't for me. So I was drifting along. I was working at Starbucks as a barista, working at a beach club as a cabana boy, setting up chairs, umbrellas, making sure the pool was tidy, that kind of thing. And studying copywriting because I wanted something better. But I was afraid to move forward. And finally I just got sick and tired of being where I was. So I came up with a genius plan to get my career started. My plan was I was living in Boca Raton at the time, and I realized that AWAI's offices were nearby. So I figured what better client to start out with. So I picked a day where I had days off from the three jobs, because I was also teaching dance privately. That was the third job. Failed to mention that. And I put on a suit. It was like 9 a.m. so I went right after they had opened up. And I just walked in the door at AWAI, turned to my right to the first person I saw and just blurted out, hey I've been studying your course and I want to work here. And I think Katie was in the background kind of like telling Jill, the lady who I turned to in a costume with my declaration of wanting to work there. Katie was like, no, no, no. Because I think Katie thought I was going to sell her a water softening system or maybe like steak and lobster out of the back of a truck. But she at least, she gave me like two minutes, talked with me, asked me if you, yeah. You did. I got Katie to join the Beach Club. So she gave me a spec assignment. I did that. And two months went by I didn't hear anything until I finally got an email from her. I guess they did a good job on that spec. And next thing I knew I was working at AWAI as a full-time copywriter on staff. And then a year later I went freelance and that brought a whole new set of fears. So basically fear will leave you to your dream life with copywriting. It will, when fear is a healthy thing that you follow, it becomes like a guidepost on the path to your dreams. And what most people do is they start off, they're at the beginning, so they see that their end goal is over there, so they start walking down the path. They buy the courses, come to the seminars, and then there's this thing called fear. And so what most people do is see it, turn around, and walk the other way, complete opposite direction of where they want to go. So what I want to convey to you guys is that you need to just go through that fear. And then when you see the next fear far down ahead, that should be a good sign, and that's where you need to go. Because you can have all the tips and strategies, but if you don't use them, nothing's going to happen. So ultimately the question is what fear is standing in your way? Because whatever you want in life for the most part, what's standing between you and what you want is simply fear. So here are three steps for following your fear. Number one, get honest with yourself. That basically comes down to sometimes we'll make up excuses, and what's underneath those excuses is just fear. For me, there was numerous times I decided I'd start my copywriting career. I thought I'd cold call clients, so I'd set the time. I said I'll start cold calling at 9. So the day came, it's 9 o'clock, I'm like, well, you know, it's morning time, they just got into the office. They don't want to hear from me, I'll call in the afternoon. 5 p.m. would come, you know, they're getting ready to go home, I don't want to bug them. I'll start in January, because that's when they have other marketing plans and they're getting ready to do stuff. So that was me, until I finally got honest with myself. All those were just excuses. It came down to fear and fear of wanting to... fear of just getting rejection, wondering if I was good enough. So to get honest, drop your excuses, identify the fear that's stopping you that's behind all those excuses, and then accept it, there's no shame at all in admitting it. And then step two is just go ahead and do it. Now, sometimes that's easier said than done, so what can help? And it's helped me. Prepare, but not so much, it becomes a crutch. Visualize, take baby steps if you need to, so if coming to in-person things or speaking in public is tough for you, start with baby steps, so maybe use email, maybe set up a website and try to get clients to come to you and do content marketing, but slowly work your way to that. And the third tip is to act fast before you can talk yourself out of it. So maybe just count to three and once you hit one, just go one, two, three, but when you say one, just go do it. And then step three, when you can't bring yourself to do it, and that might be oftentimes, as I've experienced, you need to start working on yourself and what you need to do is be willing to question the thoughts, beliefs and emotions that are causing you to fear the thing that's holding you back. So you just need to be willing to question them and, like for example, I waited months on end to raise my rates. I knew it was time, but there was a fear of what if, all those what ifs. And I couldn't bring myself to do it, so I had to work on this kind of stuff before I was actually courageous enough to do it, and it ultimately came down to not seeing my own value, and so how could clients see that value? So by working on these beliefs and identifying what was holding me back, I was able to move forward. Now I'd like to give you guys a five step action plan that can help you. It's time for a little cliche. If I can do it, you guys can too, trust me. Trust me, because many of you are nowhere near as introverted as I am. Have a much easier time talking to people, so there's no reason why you guys can't do it. So write down three things that you know would help move your copywriting business forward, and you guys have gotten tons of great tips from Clayton and all the other million dollar writers. Then pick the scariest one, the biggest one that you know really would just move it forward if you just did it. If you can't do it, then just do it. If you can't bring yourself to do it, then start doing the inner work. Kind of like Clayton talked about yesterday, identify the beliefs, what filters are you viewing that through, realize that those thoughts are simply not true ultimately, and you can choose to let them go. So for example, start questioning it. So if you're afraid of getting rejected, ask yourself what kind of person gets rejected? What does that mean about me as a person? And that will lead you to the belief that you're holding that's been stopping you. And once you realize that it's just a belief, you can begin to change it. Now some parting thoughts. You bring who you are to whatever it is you're doing. So if you don't work on this stuff, you're going to get that effect where you just live the same year again and again, and it translates to all areas of your life. So if you're somebody who always avoids fear, it's going to show up in your copywriting business too. And learning the strategies and techniques is the first half of it, but actually doing them is the second half. And getting over whatever fear is stopping you is that key, I think, to getting to where you want to be. So in order to grow your copywriting business, and what's helped me is just practice over and over and over overcoming that fear. And when you do, you'll feel great. You'll feel a release of energy. It'll build your confidence and your courageousness, and you'll slowly grow into the kinds of fears you can overcome. So it'll build little by little. And then some bonus tips. Can you tell him a copywriter? Treat it like play. Make it a game. Make it fun. When we play games, when we play board games or a sport, we're not worried about taking a risk. We're not worried about what will they think. You normally just go for it because it's a game. As soon as you start treating it way too seriously, that's when all these fears start coming up. And that's what I've learned over the five years I've been a freelance copywriter. Don't take yourself too seriously. I'm still working on that one. But, you know, you guys can start working on it too if that's an issue. And realize it's a process. It's a process. You're not going to just overcome the fear in one day, and then you're just going to be able to repeat it, and you're going to be magically cured. It's going to be a process, like I said. So you'll build by overcoming a small fear, then a slightly bigger one, slightly bigger one, slightly bigger one, until ultimately you're where you want to be. And it's a process that never ends. Or at least it shouldn't end, so you can always keep improving. And I leave you with a quote here. Only those who will risk going too far can possibly find out how far they can go. Just something to think about. Thank you.
